data.title: Canada's retail sales likely fell 0.5% in December
data.description:
• Retail sales in Canada decreased 0.50 percent in December of 2025 over the previous month. 
  • Retail Sales in Canada increased 3.10 percent in November of 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

data.title: Cisco-backed World Labs in talks to raise funding at $5B valuation: report
data.description:
• Spatial computing firm World Labs, which is backed by Cisco (CSCO), among others, is in talks with investors to raise additional funding that would value the firm at $5B, Bloomberg reported.
  • The new funding round could be for as much as $500M, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. However, the deal is not yet finalized, and the size and number of investors could change, the people added. 
  • World Labs did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Founded by world-renowned computer scientist Dr. Fei-Fei Li, World Labs is creating AI tools that can navigate the three-dimensional world. It has already released its first product, Marble, which it calls a “a first-in-class generative multimodal world model.” Marble is the company's world model that can create 3-D worlds from image or text prompts.
  • Dr. Li, who has been referred to as the “godmother of AI,” has extensive experience in the tech industry. She previously served on the board of directors at Twitter (now known as X), as an executive at Google Cloud and various other companies.

data.title: JPMorgan joins the bull camp on Procter & Gamble
data.description:
Procter & Gamble (PG) pushed higher in early trading on Friday after JPMorgan upgraded the household products giant to an Overweight rating after having it slotted at Neutral.

Analyst Andrea Teixeira and her team believe the company is poised to accelerate organic sales growth, improve margin rates in the medium term, and see re-rating  back to historical valuation multiples.

"We believe there is upside to consensus longer term as categories are recovering growth in the U.S. We believe PG will regain market share given its strong brand equity, marketing capabilities, and supply chain excellence/resilience positively in the current operating environment," wrote Texeira.

While cognizant of the slump in consumption, especially in the developed markets, the firm thinks intervention from P&G (PG) management will play a large role in the top line since the company tends to perform ahead of the market given its robust innovation. "Furthermore, PG plays in everyday essentials categories, alluding to consumers returning eventually, and PG remains one of the higher-quality names among our HPC coverage," updated Texeira.

JPMorgan assigned a price target of $165 to P&G (PG) based on a 50-50 blend of a P/E multiple of 23.1X and an EV/EBITDA multiple of 15.0X.

Shares of Procter & Gamble (PG) were up 1.2% in premarket trading after rising 2.7% yesterday on the heels of its FQ2 earnings report.

data.title: Samsung denies rumors of memory price increase by 80%: report
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) noted that rumors are inaccurate that it has implemented an 80% price increase across its memory products, United Daily News reported.

Samsung's distributors have issued price increase notices because of changes in the global semiconductor market, including supply constraints and a sharp rise in upstream manufacturing costs, the report added, as per a Google translation.

However, memory module manufacturers interviewed noted that they have not yet received any notification of a price increase, the report noted. Another module manufacturer indicated that Samsung memory prices would rise, but the manufacturer has not provided specific figures for the increase.

Samsung did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Earlier this month, it was reported that Samsung and SK hynix (HXSC.F) are looking to raise prices for server memory by up to 70% in the first quarter, as a surging demand in AI impacts global supply. South Korean companies Samsung and SK hynix are among the largest memory chipmaking companies.

In November 2025, it was reported that memory suppliers like Samsung, SK hynix and Micron Technology (MU) were already facing shortages of older dynamic RAM, or DRAM, products after reducing production to focus on high-bandwidth memory, or HBM, which is necessary to make the advanced accelerators for AI. SK hynix is a major supplier of HBM chips to Nvidia (NVDA).

data.title: Melco Resorts & Entertainment attracts a bull rating from Texas Capital
data.description:
Melco Resorts & Entertainment Limited (MLCO) is on watch after being initiated by Texas Capital Securities with a Buy rating.

Analyst David Bain thinks Melco Resorts (MLCO) unique non-gaming offerings and premium mass focus align the company with record Macau visitation and a K-shaped China economy.

Melco Resorts (MLCO) is seen as having cleaner market opportunities in the new Macau setup. "We believe the near elimination of junkets reduces market volatility and other junket-related risks. It also offers uncaptured GGR margin opportunities. Premium mass player reinvestment is currently elevated. Heightened promotional periods in Macau historically revert to rational levels," highlighted Bain. He noted that newer opex related to non-gaming and events should continue to drive visitation, diversify, and grow GGR, leading to absolute EBITDA gains.

The sell-off in MCLO shares is seen by Texas Capital as overdone and presents investors with a strong entry point. The firm's price target of $11.50 represents upside potential of more than 80%. Melco Resorts (MLC) is expected to issue its fourth-quarter earnings report at the end of February.

data.title: UBS plans to offer crypto investment options to select clients - report
data.description:
UBS Group (UBS) is reportedly selecting partners for its proposed offering of cryptocurrency investment options to some private ​banking clients.

The Swiss bank may initially allow select clients of its private bank in Switzerland to buy and ‌sell bitcoin (BTC-USD) and ether (ETH-USD), and may expand the services to Asia-Pacific and U.S. markets at a later stage, people ‌familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News.

UBS's increased focus on crypto comes in the wake of higher demand for digital assets from wealthy clients, the January 23 report noted.
